holdings.rationale: Finally, the Fund’s holding in gold bullion performed strongly, rising +39% in sterling terms, with the precious metal benefitting from simmering geopolitical risk and record central bank demand. We continue to view gold as essential portfolio insurance.

holdings.rationale: Finally, the Fund’s holding in gold bullion performed strongly, rising +39% in sterling terms, with the precious metal benefitting from simmering geopolitical risk and record central bank demand. We continue to view gold as essential portfolio insurance.

holdings.name: Unilever PLC
holdings.rationale: Unilever, the Fund’s largest equity holding, performed well. The stock was up +24.5% for the year to the end of January 2025, contributing +1% to returns. We believe there is more to come from Unilever despite the modest re-rating of the company’s valuation.

holdings.name: Alphabet Inc
holdings.rationale: Other strong performers throughout the year included Alphabet... We also added to the Fund’s holding in Alphabet. Alphabet’s valuation remains attractive, reflecting general concerns around regulation and the impact of generative AI on the company’s core search business. We believe these fears are overblown and take comfort in Alphabet’s strong operational performance. Growth in search remains strong, whilst revenue and margin progression at Google Cloud has a long way to run. As a result, Alphabet should be able to continue to grow earnings at healthy double-digit rates in the medium term.

holdings.name: United States Treasury Note/Bond 2.88% 06/15/2025

holdings.name: United States Treasury Inflation Indexed Bonds 0.13% 04/15/2026

holdings.name: Diageo PLC
holdings.rationale: We added to the Fund’s holdings in Heineken, Diageo and Alphabet... Weak organic growth and poor operational performance, alongside concerns that weight loss drugs will detrimentally impact consumption, has dented investor confidence in Diageo’s prospects. A new CEO has added to a general sense of uncertainty. We believe these issues are transitory in nature and view the fall in Diageo’s shares as an opportunity to add to a high-quality company, with leading global brands, at an attractive valuation.

holdings.name: Nestle SA
holdings.rationale: The detractors were the Fund’s other Consumer Staples businesses, especially Nestlé, whose shares have been weak since we reduced the holding at the beginning of 2022... We believe that a refocused business, combined with an attractive valuation, should lead to compelling shareholder returns over time.

holdings.name: VeriSign Inc
holdings.rationale: In terms of portfolio activity, the Fund started two new holdings in Verisign and Chubb reflecting our desire to add attractive stock-specific opportunities when they arise. Verisign is the exclusive registry for .com and .net internet domain names... We expect Verisign to grow sales and profit margins through modest growth in the number of web addresses, gradual price rises and operating leverage.

holdings.name: Heineken NV
holdings.rationale: We added to the Fund’s holdings in Heineken... Heineken continues to contend with macroeconomic headwinds in end-markets such as Nigeria and Vietnam. However, we remain confident in the strength of the management team and the business’s ability to grow its cash flows attractively over the long run.

Chubb is the largest property & casualty (P&C) insurer in the world... we expect Chubb to continue growing book value per share at a healthy rate in future.

extracted_from: Schedule of investments (unaudited) As at 31 January 2025
extraction_notes: All holdings from the 'Schedule of investments' on page 58 are included, as no threshold for 'top holdings' was specified. Sector information is inferred from the Investment Manager's report on page 6 where available, or left null if not explicitly mentioned or clearly inferable as a GICS-like sector.
